title: Love-Laurel
content:
(In Memory of Henry Kendall)


В В В В Ah! that God once would touch my lips with song
В В В В To pierce, as prayer doth heaven, earthпїЅs breast of iron,
В В В В So that with sweet mouth I might sing to thee,
В В В В O sweet dead singer buried by the sea,
В В В В A song, to woo thee, as a wooing siren,
В В В В Out of that silent sleep which seals too long
В В В В Thy mouth of melody.

В В В В For, if live lips might speak awhile to dead,
В В В В Or any speech could reach the sad world under
В В В В This world of ours, song surely should awake
В В В В Thee who didst dwell in shadow for songпїЅs sake!
В В В В Alas! thou canst not hear the voice of thunder,
В В В В Nor low dirge over thy low-lying head
В В В В The winds of morning make.

В В В В Down through the clay there comes no sound of these;
В В В В Down in the grave there is no sign of Summer,
В В В В Nor any knowledge of the soft-eyed Spring;
В В В В But Death sits there, with outspread ebon wing,
В В В В Closing with dust the mouth of each new-comer
В В В В To that mute land, where never sound of seas
В В В В Is heard, and no birds sing.

В В В В Now thou hast found the end of all thy days
В В В В Hast thou found any heart a vigil keeping
В В В В For thee among the dead, some heart that heard
В В В В Thy singing when thou wert a brown, sweet bird
В В В В Gray пїЅons gone, in some old forest sleeping
В В В В Beneath the seas long since? in DeathпїЅs dim ways
В В В В Has thy heart any word?

В В В В For surely those in whom the deathless spark
В В В В Of song is kindled, sang from the beginning
В В В В If life were always? But the old desires,
В В В В Do they exist when sad-eyed Hope expires?
В В В В How live the dead? what crowns have they for winning?
В В В В Have they, to warm them in the dreamless dark,
В В В В For sun earthпїЅs central fires?

В В В В Are the dead dead indeed whom we call dead?
В В В В Has God no life but this of ours for giving?
В В В В When that they took thee by each well-known place,
В В В В Stark in thy coffin with a cold white face,
В В В В What thought, O Brother, hadst thou of the living?
В В В В What of the sun that round thee glory shed?
В В В В What of the fair dayпїЅs grace?

В В В В Is thy new life made up of memories
В В В В Or dreams that lull the dead, bright visions bringing
В В В В Of Spring above! Are thy days short or long?
В В В В Thou who wert master of our singing throng
В В В В Mayhap in death thou hast not lost thy singing,
В В В В But chauntst unheard, beside the moaning sea,
В В В В A solitary song.

В В В В The chance spade turns up skulls. God help the dead
В В В В And thee whose singing days have all passed over,
В В В В Thee, whom the gold-haired Spring shall seek in vain
В В В В When at the glad yearпїЅs doors she stands again,
В В В В Remembering the song-garlands thou hast wove her
В В В В In years gone by: but all these years have fled
В В В В With all their joy and pain.
В В В В .В В В В  .В В В В  .В В В В  .В В В В  .
В В В В My soul laughed out to hear my heart speak so,
В В В В And sprang forth skyward, as an eagle, hoping
В В В В To look upon thy soul with living eyes,
В В В В Until it came to where our dim life dies,
В В В В And dead suns darkly for a grave are groping
В В В В Through cycles of immeasurable woe,
В В В В Stone-blind in the blind skies.

В В В В The stars walk shuddering on that awful verge
В В В В From which my soul, with swift and fearless motion,
В В В В Clove the black depths, and sought for God and thee;
В В В В But God dwells where nor stars nor suns there be,
В В В В No shore there is to His Eternal Ocean;
В В В В A thousand systems are a fringe of surge
В В В В On that great starless sea.

В В В В And thou wert not. So that, with weary plumes,
В В В В My soul through the great void its way came winging
В В В В To earth again. пїЅWhat hope for him who sings
В В В В Is there?пїЅ it sighed. пїЅDeath ends all sweetest things.пїЅ
В В В В When lo! there came a swell of mighty singing,
В В В В Flooding all space, and swift athwart the glooms
В В В В A flash of sudden wings.
В В В В .В В В В  .В В В В  .В В В В  .В В В В  .
В В В В Dreamer of dreams, thy songs and dreams are done.
В В В В Down where thou sleepest in earthпїЅs secret bosom
В В В В There is no sorrow and no joy for thee,
В В В В Who canst not see what stars at eve there be,
В В В В Nor evermore at morn the green dawn blossom
В В В В Into the golden king-flower of the sun
В В В В Across the golden sea.

В В В В But haply there shall come in days to be
В В В В One who shall hear his own heart beating faster,
В В В В Plucking a rose sprung from thy heart beneath,
В В В В And from his soul, as sword from out its sheath,
В В В В Song shall leap forth where now, O silent master,
В В В В On thy lone grave beside the sounding sea,
В В В В I lay this laurel-wreath.
meaning: About: Victor James William Patrick Daley (5 September 1858 пїЅ 29 December 1905) was an Australian poet and journalist.
